observations.correspondence_rationale: The text explicitly references the biblical story of Lot's wife, who looked back at the destruction of Sodom and was turned into a pillar of salt, symbolizing the consequences of disobedience and the inability to move forward.
observations.summary: The narrative alludes to the story of Lot's wife, who was turned into salt for looking back, symbolizing the danger of dwelling on the past and the necessity of moving forward.

evidence.quote: Looking back turned you into salt, froze you in place, made you a monument to your own inability to move forward.
